div.h1: Van Emde Boas (vEB) Layout: Recursive Construction

p.strong: Height-4 Tree (15 nodes) split at middle height (H/2 = 2)
p.em: Cache-oblivious data structure for optimal search performance.

ul.li: Any search path from root to leaf crosses $O(\log N)$ nodes.
vEB ensures that any sub-tree of size $B$ (cache block size) is stored contiguously.
Memory Transfers: $O(\log_B N)$, matching the optimal B-Tree without knowing $B$.
Reduces cache misses by up to 75% compared to standard BFS/DFS layouts.

div.p: 1. Recursive Split Split tree of height $H$ at $H/2$. This creates one 'Top' subtree and $\sqrt{N}$ 'Bottom' subtrees.
2. Store Top Cluster Recursively lay out the Top subtree. These nodes occupy the first memory slots.
3. Store Bottom Clusters Lay out each Bottom subtree recursively. Store them left-to-right in the array.
